async-keyword.rationale: `async`/`await` is the sugar that hides the erased error channel of `Promise<T>`.

promise-type.rationale: `Promise<T>` erases the error channel; tagged-error or `Effect<T, E, R>` keep it visible.

then-chain.rationale: `.then()` chains hide the error channel and reorder control flow; same erasure as `Promise<T>`.

bare-catch.rationale: A silent catch hides both the error AND the branch; exhaustiveness cannot apply.

record-cast.rationale: `as Record<string, unknown>` papers over a missing schema at the boundary; decode instead.

no-raw-sql.rationale: Raw SQL defeats the compiler; a typed builder makes the schema load-bearing at compile time.

no-manual-enum-cast.rationale: Hand-written unions drift from their source; generated or schema-derived enums do not.

no-vitest-mocks.rationale: An integration test that mocks the boundary asserts your code works against your mock, not the real thing.

no-hardcoded-secrets.rationale: A hardcoded secret bypasses the env-boundary schema; secrets must be decoded at boot, not embedded in source.

no-raw-throw-new-error.rationale: `throw new Error("bad")` has no type, no handling contract, no receipt for the caller; tagged errors do.

no-test-skip-only.rationale: Test-hygiene corollary of principle 1: a `.skip` or `.only` shipped to main is a test that does not test.

no-coverage-threshold-gate.rationale: Coverage % is a diagnostic, never a CI gate (Inozemtseva & Holmes ICSE 2014). Acts on the wrong signal.

no-hardcoded-assertion-literals.rationale: Assertions hardcoding a literal (e.g., `expect(...).toBe(42)` where 42 is the implementation's output) compress the same information as a property; the property has higher coverage.
